$comment: Twenty queries phrased the way someone would actually ask them, each paired
with the chunks that genuinely answer it. Labelled by hand: candidates were
surfaced from the store, then read and kept or discarded one at a time.
Chunks are named by their content-addressed `id`, never by position, so
re-ingesting the corpus does not silently invalidate this file. If a chunk's
own text is edited its id changes and it drops out of the gold set — which is
the intended behaviour: the labelling was for the text as it read at the time.
The gold sets are deliberately conservative. A passage is listed only if it
answers the question, not merely if it mentions the subject, so recall is
measured against material a reader would actually be glad to receive.

queries.query: How does Conway's Game of Life decide whether a cell lives or dies?

What are elementary cellular automata?
How does a simple rule create emergent complexity?
What is the structure of the IELTS exam?
How is the TOEFL iBT organised?
Which Cambridge English exams are there?
What should I know about React hooks?
Explain closures in JavaScript
How should I approach a system design interview?
How does the Node.js event loop work?
What does an Angular developer need to learn?
